session.assistant:
I can see the issue now. The production code at line 355 outputs `"plan_backend": "planned_pr"` in the JSON. But the test was changed to assert `output["pr_backend"]`. The test is now asserting the wrong key. This is an incorrect rename - the output JSON key `"plan_backend"` is part of the external API (like the GitHub Actions workflow inputs).
Let me check what the test_plan_save.py asserts:

54→def test_planned_pr_success_json(tmp_path: Path, monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch) -> None: 55→ """Happy path: exit 0, JSON output has success/issue_number/branch_name.""" 56→ ctx = _planned_pr_context(tmp_path=tmp_path, monkeypatch=monkeypatch) 57→ runner = CliRunner() 58→ 59→ result = runner.invoke(plan_save, ["--format", "json", "--branch-slug", "test-slug"], obj=ctx) 60→ 61→ assert result.exit_code == 0, f"Failed: {result.output}" 62→ output = json.loads(result.output) 63→ assert output["success"] is True 64→ assert "pr_number" in output 65→ assert "branch_name" in output 66→ assert output["branch_name"].startswith("plnd/") 67→ assert output["pr_backend"] == "planned_pr" 68→ 69→ 70→def test_planned_pr_success_display(tmp_path: Path, monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch) -> None: 71→ """Display format: output contains 'Plan saved as planned PR'.""" 72→ ctx = _planned_pr_context(tmp_path=tmp_path, monkeypatch=monkeypatch) 73→ runner = CliRunner()

cwd=tmp_path,\\n repo_root=tmp_path,\\n )\"},\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_01Wu8yBTGQWtsK4CDnwTrTfo\"}", /home/runner/work/erk/erk/CHANGELOG.md:512:**How it works:** Plans can now be stored as GitHub draft PRs instead of issues, with `ERK_PLAN_BACKEND=draft_pr` controlling the backend. All workflows (plan-save, plan-implement, learn, CI) support both backends transparently. /home/runner/work/erk/erk/CHANGELOG.md:514:**Key features:** `ERK_PLAN_BACKEND` environment variable, draft PR auto-publishing during `erk pr submit`, backend-agnostic plan checkout via `erk br co --for-plan`. /home/runner/work/erk/erk/CHANGELOG.md:594:- Simplify plan backend configuration from 3-tier to 2-tier: `ERK_PLAN_BACKEND` env var or default, removing config-file tier /home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/testing/tripwires.md:73:**debugging 100+ unexpected test failures with no obvious cause** → Read [Environment Variable Isolation in Tests](environment-variable-isolation.md) first. Check ERK_PLAN_BACKEND first. Although the env var is now obsolete (get_plan_backend() was deleted in PR #7971), legacy code paths in context_for_test() may still read it. Use monkeypatch.delenv('ERK_PLAN_BACKEND', raising=False) or env_overrides={} in fixtures as a defensive measure until full cleanup in objective #7911. /home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/testing/tripwires.md:109:**testing code that reads ERK_PLAN_BACKEND or other environment variables via CliRunner** → Read [CLI Testing Patterns](cli-testing.md) first. CliRunner env var isolation: ambient env vars from the developer shell leak into CliRunner by default and cause intermittent test failures. Use CliRunner(env={'ERK_PLAN_BACKEND': '...'}) to override, or CliRunner(env={}) to isolate completely. Never rely on ambient env being clean. Note: mix_stderr parameter is broken in Click 8.3.1 — do not use it. /home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/testing/cli-testing.md:10: - action: "testing code that reads ERK_PLAN_BACKEND or other environment variables via CliRunner" /home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/testing/cli-testing.md:11: warning: "CliRunner env var isolation: ambient env vars from the developer shell leak into CliRunner by default and cause intermittent test failures. Use CliRunner(env={'ERK_PLAN_BACKEND': '...'}) to override, or CliRunner(env={}) to isolate completely. Never rely on ambient env being clean. Note: mix_stderr parameter is broken in Click 8.3.1 — do not use it." /home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/testing/index.md:17:- **[environment-variable-isolation.md](environment-variable-isolation.md)** — debugging systematic test failures across many test files, working with ERK_PLAN_BACKEND in tests, understanding why tests behave differently based on environment, writing tests that involve plan backend selection /home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/testing/environment-variable-isolation.md:5: - "working with ERK_PLAN_BACKEND in tests" /home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/testing/environment-variable-isolation.md:10: warning: "Check ERK_PLAN_BACKEND first. Although the env var is now obsolete (get_plan_backend() was deleted in PR #7971), legacy code paths in context_for_test() may still read it. Use monkeypatch.delenv('ERK_PLAN_BACKEND', raising=False) or env_overrides={} in fixtures as a defensive measure until full cleanup in objective #7911." /home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/testing/environment-variable-isolation.md:16:## The `ERK_PLAN_BACKEND` Contamination Pattern /home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/testing/environment-variable-isolation.md:18:> **Note:** After PR #7971 (objective #7911 node 1.1), the `get_plan_backend()` function was deleted and the plan backend is hardcoded to `"planned_pr"`. The `ERK_PLAN_BACKEND` environment variable is no longer read by application code. The contamination pattern described below is historical but the mitigations remain relevant until vestigial code paths are fully cleaned up in later nodes of objective #7911. /home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/testing/environment-variable-isolation.md:20:Previously, setting `ERK_PLAN_BACKEND=planned_pr` in the shell environment caused **125+ test failures** when running the full test suite. /home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/testing/environment-variable-isolation.md:24:`context_for_test()` in `tests/fakes/tests/shared_context.py` creates a test `ErkContext`. After PR #7971, the plan backend selection uses a tautological comparison that always takes the planned-PR path, regardless of environment variables. Tests that set `ERK_PLAN_BACKEND` are now exercising dead code paths. Monkeypatching this variable has no behavioral effect. /home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/testing/environment-variable-isolation.md:32:After PR #8210, `context_for_test()` in `tests/fakes/tests/shared_context.py` no longer checks `ERK_PLAN_BACKEND`. The plan backend is hardcoded to `ManagedGitHubPrBackend`.
session.assistant:
This is very helpful. According to `docs/learned/testing/environment-variable-isolation.md`, `ERK_PLAN_BACKEND` is **already obsolete** - the env var is no longer read by application code. Setting `monkeypatch.setenv("ERK_PR_BACKEND", ...)` has no behavioral effect either way. Those test setenv calls are testing dead code paths.
So the `ERK_PR_BACKEND` rename in the tests is harmless (the env var isn't read anywhere), but the `output["pr_backend"]` assertion IS wrong - it's asserting a JSON output key that the production code still emits as `"plan_backend"`.
I need to revert those two assertions in `test_plan_save.py` back to `output["plan_backend"]`. Also check the dispatch tests that changed `inputs["plan_backend"]` → `inputs["pr_backend"]` since the workflow inputs use `"plan_backend"`:
